2ABOUT TECHNO BRAIN
AFRICA USA MIDDLE EAST INDIA UK
Techno Brain is a leading digital Technology firm providing products and services across regions and empowering lives 
through innovation and skills transfer with operations in Africa, USA, India, UK and UAE.
Techno Brain is committed to deliver high quality, cost effective and off-the-shelf solutions to Governments, NGOs and Private 
organizations across the globe.

3Trips+ is a COTS solution designed to help 
Revenue Authorities deliver performance 
improvements and better services to taxpayers, 
through online and front office service portals 
using state of the art open source and Microsoft 
technologies.
Trips+ solution provides the flexibility of 
delivering pre-defined process templates 
out of the box for the standard Revenue 
Administration functionality. The solution 
proposed is based on a ‘core’ concept of 
Revenue Document processing, coupled with 
a Risk Analysis engine for compliance, and a 
Case Management engine for workflow, task 
management and scheduling. Trips+ also integrates a reporting engine and a secure 
Document Management engine.
Trips+ Integrated Document Management 
module provides a secure and robust platform 
where all documents and notices are stored 
and accessed only via Trips+ application user 
interfaces and processes.
Trips+ Integration with external systems is 
achieved through secure web service interfaces. 
As these are generally complex and very 
specific activities, these areas of the system are 
customized to support secure access and are 
not generally user maintained once defined.
Trips+ Application Stack
Core ITAS Business Intelligence & Reporting 
Services
Taxpayer services Chabot and RPA
Common Business Services Biometric Registration

4Trips+ Modules
Trips+ provides Taxpayers and Tax Authorities with the following modules


5Taxpayer Portal
Taxpayers accessing Trips+ online services are supported by the taxpayer portal framework which 
is a part of the eService in two main ways:
By providing information and assistance 
to the Taxpayer through multiple channels 
such as mobile, webTrips+ enables Taxpayers to conduct their 
business 24/7 through multiple channels - 
the web, smart mobile and voice.
Information can be easily created, delivered, 
searched for and categorized. The portal 
allows for the updating of tax payer 
information. Content can be made available 
to taxpayers through FAQ’s or document/
library search functions. This enables the 
authority to push specific information to 
target user groups.
Trips+ provides meaningful error messages 
that guides users on what might have gone 
wrong By enabling the Taxpayer to transact 
business with the tax authority 24/7 from 
anywhere in the world
The taxpayer portal is integrated with 
the Trips+ core services.  To the user, the 
integration is seamless. They simply access 
the portal and select the menu options 
required for the actions they wish to achieve.  
Taxpayer documents submitted on-line are 
seamlessly transmitted from the portal to 
the core services. 
Taxpayer Portal Features
The system provides secure access controls 
to restrict access to parts of the system to 
authorized users. Transactions performed 
using the internet are secured using SSL 
encryption.1

Taxpayers can pay their liabilities 
electronically using several different 
methods such as mobile, cash card,

card payments, receiving a uniquely

been received. 
The Taxpayer is able to upload data from 
their system into ITAS through the portal 
upload facility 
 
Taxpayers can appoint an authorized agent act on their behalf using the appropriate registration 
process. on the portal. Taxpayers or their agents can view their tax ledger online and drill down to 
view each transaction detail as required.
Data validation is carried out in the portal, whilst all the processing is performed in Trips+ core 
services. This integration enables the taxpayer self-service experience to be tailored to the needs of 
the Taxpayer, whilst delivering the core business process.

7Trips+ e-Services
Trips+ e-Services delivers industry standard 
RESTful Web API services with secure 
authentication and identity protocols.
Trips+ provides a centralized Taxpayer 
Registry of the highest integrity. To 
maintain the integrity of the registry, 
sophisticated and automated error 
checking, validation, risk management, 
and audit functionality is provided 
through workflow. 
The registration process is simple, 
immediate and customer friendly, 
with the Taxpayer receiving immediate 
validation and receipt of the submission.  
Taxpayers can register via multiple 
channels, such as in person, by letter, 
mobile or web and nominates via their 
preferred communication method e.g. 
post, email, web portal inbox. As part of 
the registration workflow, the application 
is automatically cross checked for 
duplicates in both its own and any third-
party database (such as business or civil 
registries) before acceptance. Taxpayer Registration

8The system is flexible in its business rules. It enables the authority to define the information that 
is captured and what elements are mandatory or optional. In its standard form, Trips+ records all 
industry standard data and has the flexibility to easily add additional elements that may be unique 
to the authority
When the initial registration application is 
approved, Trips+ generates a unique TIN, 
advises the Taxpayer through automated 
notices and provides the necessary 
certification.Additional information is obtained 
during subsequent Taxpayer registration 
interactions, for example when providing a 
business registration form, nominating an 
agent or during the process of registering 
for a specific tax type.
Trips+  enables the full registration of individuals, corporations and other legal entities and will 
keep track of taxpayer status.
Trips+  security functions enable the use of both Digital Signature certificates and secure tokens. 
Figure 1 - Taxpayer Registration Back Office

Trips+ provides multiple input screens 
capturing the following information
Payment Processing 
Trips+ supports electronic payments through 
the Portal, Other Payment Gateways and 
Banks. 
Taxpayers can pay their liabilities using several 
different methods such as mobile, bank, inter-
bank transfer and debit/credit card payments, 
mobile money, point-of-sale using secure 
industry standard protocols and architecture 
such as RESTful, XML and JSON. A uniquely numbered, date and time stamped receipt is 
generated for each payment made.Payments 
are posted to a Taxpayer’s ledgers and used 
to offset outstanding tax-type liabilities in 
accordance with tax administration’s business 
rules. The system allows a single payment to 
offset several outstanding tax-type liabilities.  
Credits paid in advance of a liability are lodged 
in the Taxpayer’s account and disbursed in 
accordance with tax administration’s business 
rules (for example, to offset a liability when this 
is received). 
Payments that cannot be identified are 
placed in a suspense account and notified to 
an authorized officer for reconciliation. Trips+ 
business intelligence functionality enables the 
generation of Management Information across 
all aspects of the payment process. Data can 
be generated in many formats and timescales, 
according to the wishers of the Tax Authority.Identification
Employment Details
Occupation Business Interests
Addresses
Tax Types
Contact Methods
Notes
History

Attachments
Relationships
Commercial Vehicles
Property Details

10Reverse Payment  
Payments can never be physically deleted from the system, but the system’s accounting facility 
does provide the ability to reverse out a payment if it doesn’t clear.
ComplianceDebt Management
Debt management and the ability to track 
and monitor Taxpayer debts is a key function 
within Trips+ .  It is essential for the support of 
enforcement, debt recovery, bankruptcy and 
liquidation proceedings, criminal prosecution 
of tax offences and the checking of tax debts 
required by other Government departments. 
A Taxpayer account selected for debt 
management by the system will automatically 
have enforcement action initiated in 
accordance with the authority’s business rules 
and a Debt Case created and assigned to the 
designated officer in the Debt Management 
Team.
The system supports the Debt Management 
Team to manage the case by delivering the following functionality: 
Generating notices, 
Enabling third party documents to be 
uploaded (such as court documents) and 
Configurable case SLA’s with escalation 
actions
Recording actions and outcomes during 
the collection exercise
Scheduling meeting room resources
Sending and receiving email 
communication linked to the case
Creating tasks and actions for self and 
colleagues related to the case
Recording outcomes and actions agreed 
from telephone conversations
Attaching notes and other meeting 
documents related to the case
The workflow also enforces rules regarding 
supervisor authorization, such as sending 
the case to a nominated supervisor for 
review and approval.
Trips+ provides several configurable 
procedures to facilitate compliance 
enforcement. 
In terms of late or non-payment the system 
can be configured for each Tax Type to provide 
one or any combination of the following 
facilities:
The automatic generation of a series of 
reminders and demand letters;
The automatic generation of an 
automated assessment;
The automatic generation and posting of 
fixed or variable penalties;
The automatic generation of interest on 
the taxpayer’s debt;
The posting of a manual penalty. 
Trips+  based on the configured  
business rules can enforce compliance 
check before issuing out tax clearance 
certificate.
Assessment and Returns
The system enables Taxpayers to be self-reliant 
in managing their taxes. Its design allows 
Taxpayers to submit self-assessed returns 
easily and quickly, whilst providing them with well-structured support throughout the 
process. 
Upon successful submission of the return, the 
assessment is posted as a tax-type liability 
on the Taxpayer’s ledger and the Taxpayer 
notified.  Trips+ updates the Taxpayer’s filing 
compliance status, computes and assigns risk 
points to each account (for timely or late filing), 
and updates the Taxpayer’s risk score.
Authorized officers and the Taxpayer can view 
an image of the electronically filed return 
information online. Authorized officers can 
initiate an amended return business process, 
an amended return case will be created 
by the system Taxpayers, their agents, and 
authorized users can view returns online. For 
paper returns requiring manual entry after 
lodgment and receipt processing, the system 
creates tasks to ensure that additional details 
are processed on a timely basis and that SLA 
for data entry are managed 
Taxpayers can file multiple tax-type returns 
through multiple channels such as via the web 
or mobile or at the local tax office.

12A key strength of Trips+ is its risk analysis 
functionality which uses AI Risk Agents and 
expert rules. These AI agents fully integrate 
with the registration, returns processing, 
refunds, and compliance modules. By 
providing automated risk analysis and 
reporting, based on risk profiles and other 
criteria set by authorized officers, Trips+ 
enables the authority to focus its resources on 
areas of greatest non-compliance.
Trips+ produces reports, at time intervals required by the tax authority, prioritizing Taxpayers for 
audit. This is based on their aggregate risk profiles, the amount of revenue at risk or other criteria 
set by the tax administration. It also randomly selects Taxpayers for audit in accordance with the 
authority’s rules. 
These audit selection risk profiles can include benchmarking against various industry averages 
using criteria such as turnover and commercial activity code
Results that are outside the standard deviations of the mean set by the Revenue Authority 
are automatically highlighted for further analysis by nominated officials through workflow in 
accordance with the Authority’s business rules.
Audit planners can supplement this information by using the business intelligence tools and 
through interfacing with third party systems to obtain further information about the Taxpayer as 
part of final case selection. An example could be the results of data mining exercises from the data 
warehouse or intelligence from the customs system.
Trips+ provides a complete set of 
functionalities for the case management of 
Objections and Appeals. Taxpayer’s can file 
their appeals electronically via the web or in 
hard copy and their submissions are validated 
for completeness before processing. 
The system validates the appeal according the 
authority’s business rules before acceptance or 
rejection. The Taxpayer is notified if the appeal 
has been successfully lodged or, if not, what The underlying basis of all tax management 
control operations within Trips+ is driven by 
targeting and intelligence to combat fraud 
and increase compliance. The sophisticated 
query and reporting tools in Trips+ enables 
authorized users to mine information in the 
data warehouse to identify trends, investigate 
particular sectors of the economy or other 
areas of possible non-compliance.
Audit
Objections and Appeals
corrective action is required to be undertaken.
As the processes are workflow driven, all 
aspects of the unique appeals and objections 
business can be captured in Trips+.
The case management functionality supports 
the officer throughout the case, from creation 
to closure. For example, the workflow prompts 
the officer to record relevant data such as the 
date and outcome of an appeal and updates 
the status of the case at each stage. 

13The Trips+ Case Management module provides industry leading capabilities for case manage -
ment and workflows. These workflows are user maintainable and versioned, enabling the Revenue 
Authority to take control of their business processes and workflows, while providing robust security 
and audit facilities as well as task scheduling and performance metrics with ‘out-of-the-box’ dash -
boards and reporting tools.
Trips+ functionality is delivered as workflow driven business processes with many steps and deci -
sion points referenced by business rules and validation.

14Trips+ supports either Cash or Accrual 
accounting methodologies. The Accrual 
approach is the default method, whilst 
Cash is an option that is configured during 
installation.  
Trips+ implements as its Core Chart of 
Account the IMF’s GFS codes as the default 
configuration as as a standard feature. 
The ability to configure specific codes and 
structure is supported. Codes can be created 
using the general administration menu and 
there is currently no limit on the number of 
levels that can be configured. Figure: Revenue Model
Common Services
Revenue Administration
The posting process is automatic and 
performed as a back-office function. Through 
configuring the transaction posting table, 
the individual posting requirements for each 
transaction is maintained. Trips+ supports this 
business requirement through double entry 
accounting. Each transaction in the system is 
mapped to the chart of accounts for debit and 
credit postings.Integrated

15The posting process is automatic and performed as a core system function in accordance with 
the authority’s configuration and business rules. The individual posting requirements for each 
transaction are maintained by configuring the transaction-posting table.The structure of the ledger accounts for a Taxpayer is on several levels, at the top level a main 
account is created for each Taxpayer. Below the main account, a sub-account will be created for 
each Tax Type registered to the Taxpayer.
 
Below each Tax Type sub-account further Periodic Accounts will be created for each Tax Period 
within the Tax Type. 
The posting transactions that sit below each Periodic Account will hold a balance that represents 
its allocation status. If this balance is zero for a Return posting this indicates that it has been fully 
paid. If this balance is zero for Payment posting this will indicate that the payment has been fully 
allocated. Taxpayer Ledger Account Structure
Taxpayer Account Postings
Liability  - A liability represents a Tax Type debt incurred by the taxpayer which is due to be 
paid.This requires the creation of credit and debit transactions for Taxpayer Accounting.  The rules for 
the treatment of the transaction when posting is based on the Transaction Type.
The following Transaction Types are catered for:
Payment  - A Payment is made to offset outstanding current or future liabilities which are 
incurred by the Taxpayer
Refund  - A Refund is the payment to the Taxpayer of any overpayment or credit on a Tax Type 
account which is due to the Taxpayer
Adjustment  - An Adjustment is a credit or debit transaction which alters the Taxpayer’s 
account balance
Reversal  - A Reversal transaction will undo all the transaction posting actions that were 
applied when the transaction was originally posted. 
The system also provides several commonly required account adjustment functions as well 
a general-purpose ledger adjustment facility. By providing a set of commonly required and 
more specific adjustment transactions, it restricts the need to use the general-purpose ledger 
adjustment facility. 
This adjustment allows an authorized user to create a balanced debit and credit journal entry 
against one or more account, if the tax administration’s business rules allow. This type of 
transaction usually requires the approval of senior managers.Account Adjustment Entry

16Trips+ solution provides the flexibility of delivering pre-defined process templates out of the box 
for the standard Revenue Administration functionality. The solution proposed is based on a ‘core’ 
concept of Revenue Document processing, coupled with a Risk Analysis engine for compliance, a 
Case Management engine for workflow, task management and scheduling. Trips+ also integrates a 
reporting engine and a secure Document Management engine.Figure: Configuration Model
Manage Credit Allocation Between Accounts
This function in the system, allows a Revenue Officer to apply unallocated credits e.g. payments 
in the Taxpayer’s Account to any outstanding debt or liability on the ledger subject to specified 
business rules.
Manage Dishonoured Payments
This function in the system, allows a Revenue Officer to manage a Payment Instrument that has 
been dishonoured by a bank or financial institution, subject to specified business rules

and intelligence to combat fraud and increase compliance. The sophisticated query and reporting 
tools in Trips+ enable authorized users to mine information in the data warehouse to identify 
trends, investigate sectors of the economy or other areas of possible non-compliance.

viewing

19The TRIPS+ platform utilizes the base SecuRegister system to register and capture biometric 
attributes of tax-payers in a seamless way
Biometric attributes are captured and stored against each tax-payer during the registration 
process, which can then be used during transaction authentication process
The base SecuRegister platform enables capturing of multiple biometric modalities – finger, face 
and iris - enabling  strong identity provisioningProcess Innovation

Collection of Non-Tax revenues is a component that Tax Authorities key to achieve. Our own 
Intellectual Property – RevenueACA, will be able to tap into this rich source of revenue generated 
within Uganda daily through various MDAs. We offer Tax Authorities a robust platform to manage 
the non-tax component, with the ability to develop more areas as per changing legislature.
There will be a self-service portal for the 
citizens on Trips+, through seamless 
integration, Tax Authority will give them access 
to select services that they can interact with 
the MDA specific payment obligations e.g.- 
seeing bills, application of business permits, 
land rates and rentals, vehicle permit, etc. 
These services can be configured as per 
requirements of Tax Authority.Non Tax Revenue
Our proposition will cover the following
Within the consolidated Trips+ portal, they will 
also be able to proceed to either pay for the 
services via the channels we will provide i.e.

The Revenue Management component of the system will enable TAX AUTHORITY to manage all 
the revenue streams that are handled by the various MDAs
 
Below is how our solution will support revenue collection and management across TAX 
AUTHORITYa) Collection
b) Monitoring and visualization over a GIS map
c) Analysis and reporting
d) Enhancement

23
The various MDAs will be able to define an unlimited set of Non-Tax revenue sources. Each revenue 
source can be associated with a pre-defined fee-rate, for ease of tracking and accountability. This 
will ensure that all the revenues listed are managed centrally and in the system, can be added and 
modified as required by authorized personnel.
A typical revenue source card, and a list of revenue sources is shown below.Define Multiple Revenue Sources

24The MDAs can setup varying revenue collection mechanisms depending on preference. 
Currently, some revenue sources may be paid directly at the collection point via POS devices, or 
in designated banks, agents, while other types may be paid on demand using Invoices. Typical 
examples are Market fees, parking fees and Property Rates, Rentals, Business Permits etc.
Create user defined Revenue collection options

25The system can maintain a comprehensive database of Tax Payers. These can be grouped per City, 
district etc. and for each revenue type. The grouping is user defined and can be categorized as 
per TAX AUTHORITY requirements. The system can also maintain a database of these important 
aspects:
Maintain Revenue Collectors in each Area
Revenue collectors 
Property details, including assessments
For revenue collectors, it is possible to track the amounts due, the amounts collected and balances 
uncollected. See the example below or a report output from the system.
Business License module is designed to help manage the Business Licenses collection and 
automation of due rates where monitoring and decision making is much easierSingle Business Permits

26
Details like the owner is recorded while attaching the business to its categorized business types 
ensure payment due is automatically calculated by the system with no manual intervention


27The billboard module is designed to help manage all advertisement billboards and rates that are 
to be paid to the MDAs. These billboards can be tagged to either a corporate entity or citizenry 
with various parameters such as billboard location, actual coordinates (x, y), pictures and any other 
parameters that might be relevant.
This tagging will ensure proactive billing and visibility of billboards across the whole country 
enhancing enforcement and increasing revenue collection against this highly lucrative revenue 
stream.
This module will ensure that TAX AUTHORITY is able to track all the tenants in their various houses 
owned by MDAs, how much they owe, change in tenants and all relevant obligations that are due 
by the tenants.
The tenants will be able to get their due monthly rental bills and also get alerted via SMS and/or 
emails when the due dates for payment approach.Billboard Management
Rentals Management

28
Property management module is designed to help manage the collection of rates in a much more 
informed environment where monitoring is much easier.
The module is made up of the following main components.
a)Property and Land Valuation
All details in regard to the property are captured detailing the occupier, owner and addresses for 
the billing to be sent to. A history of the change of owner is also maintained and can be analyzed 
as desired.
A list of valuations and property improvement values are also maintained in this section.
Property Management

29
Property Rate Invoicing
Rate payers are invoiced at certain periods either individually or as a group collectively. Invoices 
capture the different valuations as captured in the property card detailing land and property rate/
valuation and these are calculated against the tariffs set by the MDAs.
These invoices can then be analyzed before finally posting, printing and sending them to the 
respective rate payers either by hard copy mail or electronic mail.
Periodic Activities
These are the performed once in a while. Generating of rate invoices is done at specified periods 
monthly, quarterly or annually. A due date of the invoices can also be specified and this ensures 
that the auto generated invoices output the desired combinations and due amounts to the MDAs.

- Revenue Summary reports
- Revenue Forecast reports
- Reconciliation (Cash/Bank) reports
- System Audit reports

collected and due.
